name: Lago de Truchillas Trail
name_original: Lago de Truchillas — trail running ida y vuelta por la Sierra de La Cabrera (PR-LE 2)
description: Jog the difficult 7.2-mile Lago de Truchillas Trail in Sierra de La Cabrera, gaining 1786 feet to a glacial lake.
region_intro: Jogging around Truchas offers a variety of routes through a mountainous landscape, characterized by peaks, lakes, and natural areas like Montes Aquilianos. The region features trails that traverse diverse terrain, from circular paths through Tebaida Berciana to routes around Lago de Truchillas. Elevation changes are common, providing a challenging environment for runners. Best jogging routes around Truchas The most popular jogging route is Sendero Circular de la Tebaida Berciana, a 2.8 miles (4.4 km) trail that takes about 39 minutes to complete. This circular path offers a focused running experience within a natural setting. Another top favourite among local runners is Lago de Truchillas Trail, a difficult 7.2 miles (11.6 km) path. This route features terrain around the lake, providing scenic views and varied running surfaces. Local runners also love the Pico Tuerto - trail running round trip from Puerto de los Portillinos through Montes Aquilianos, an 8.0 miles (12.8 km) trail leading through mountainous terrain, often completed in about 2 hours 8 minutes. Jogging around Truchas is defined by mountainous trails, lake-side paths, and challenging ascents, with options for moderate to difficult running experiences. The network offers routes primarily suited for experienced runners. The routes in Truchas are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.5 stars from more than 2 reviews. More than 40 runners have used komoot to explore Truchas's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: What is the general difficulty level of running routes in Truchas?
A: The running routes around Truchas are primarily suited for experienced runners, with most trails rated as difficult. There are 7 difficult routes and 2 moderate routes available, but no easy options. This mountainous region features challenging ascents and varied terrain.

Q: Are there any circular running routes in Truchas?
A: Yes, Truchas offers several circular running routes. A popular option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1052058840/sendero-circular-de-la-tebaida-berciana" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sendero Circular de la Tebaida Berciana&lt;/a&gt;, a 2.8-mile (4.4 km) difficult trail. Another circular route is &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1052061912/la-mirada-circular-natural-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Mirada Circular Natural Trail&lt;/a&gt;, which is 6.9 miles (11.1 km) long and rated as moderate.

Q: What kind of scenery can I expect on the running trails around Truchas?
A: Runners in Truchas can expect stunning mountainous landscapes, characterized by peaks, lakes, and natural areas like Montes Aquilianos. Trails often traverse diverse terrain, including lake-side paths around places like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1055954311/lago-de-truchillas-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Lago de Truchillas&lt;/a&gt;, offering scenic views throughout your run.

Q: Are there any waterfalls or other natural attractions along the running routes?
A: Yes, the region around Truchas features natural attractions. You might encounter the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5542912" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Waterfall and Bridge of the Arroyo del Silencio&lt;/a&gt;. Additionally, the area boasts highlights like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5431418" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Truchillas River Wooden Bridge&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3781415" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;San Genadio Cave&lt;/a&gt;, which add to the natural beauty of the trails.

Q: What do other runners say about the trails in Truchas?
A: The running routes in Truchas are highly regarded by the komoot community, holding an average rating of 4.5 stars from over 2 reviews. More than 40 runners have used komoot to explore the varied and challenging terrain, often praising the mountainous trails and lake-side paths.

Q: What is the longest running route available in Truchas?
A: One of the longer and more challenging running routes in the area is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31470276" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Teleno loop from La Taberna de Filiel&lt;/a&gt;. This difficult trail covers approximately 10.9 miles (17.6 km) and involves significant elevation changes.

Q: Are the running trails in Truchas suitable for families or beginners?
A: Given that most routes in Truchas are rated as difficult, and there are no easy options, the trails are generally not ideal for families with young children or absolute beginners. The terrain often includes challenging ascents and varied surfaces, better suited for experienced runners.

Q: Can I find routes that pass through villages or offer cultural sights?
A: While the focus is on natural landscapes, some routes may pass near or offer views of charming settlements. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4640996" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Peñalba de Santiago Village&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6189928" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Montes de Valdueza Village&lt;/a&gt; are notable highlights in the broader region that runners might appreciate.

Q: What is the typical duration for a running route in Truchas?
A: The duration of running routes in Truchas varies significantly based on distance and difficulty. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1052058840/sendero-circular-de-la-tebaida-berciana" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sendero Circular de la Tebaida Berciana&lt;/a&gt; takes about 39 minutes, while the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1052046643/pico-tuerto-trail-running-round-trip-from-puerto-de-los-portillinos-through-montes-aquilianos" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pico Tuerto - trail running round trip from Puerto de los Portillinos through Montes Aquilianos&lt;/a&gt; typically takes around 2 hours and 8 minutes due to its 8.0-mile (12.8 km) length and challenging elevation.

Q: Are there any specific peaks or mountain passes that runners can explore?
A: Yes, the mountainous terrain around Truchas includes several notable peaks and passes. Runners can explore areas like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5518524" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pico Tuerto (2,051 m)&lt;/a&gt;, which is featured in the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1052046643/pico-tuerto-trail-running-round-trip-from-puerto-de-los-portillinos-through-montes-aquilianos" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pico Tuerto trail running route&lt;/a&gt;. Other significant points include &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3497179" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;El Morredero Pass&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5518533" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Collado de la Silla de la Yegua&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What is the total number of running routes available in the Truchas area?
A: There are 9 distinct running routes documented in the Truchas area, offering a range of experiences primarily for more advanced runners due to the challenging mountainous terrain.
tips: Nice wooden bridge that helps us cross the Truchillas River when we walk the Path of the Lake of the same name. A delight for lovers of hiking and nature. The route is well signposted.

The surroundings of the lake are ideal for hiking and nature watching. The route to Lake Truchillas is approximately 7 kilometres long and offers spectacular views of the Eria River valley and the typical vegetation of the area, such as heather and juniper. In addition, it is possible to spot diverse fauna, including deer, wild boar and birds of prey such as the golden eagle.

faqs.question: How long does it typically take to complete the Lago de Truchillas Trail?
faqs.answer: For jogging, the Komoot data suggests an average duration of approximately 1 hour and 52 minutes. However, given its challenging nature with significant elevation gain and varied terrain, many visitors might take longer, especially if they pause to enjoy the scenery or navigate the steeper sections like the 'Cuesta Millín' at a slower pace.

faqs.question: Is the Lago de Truchillas Trail suitable for beginners?
faqs.answer: No, this trail is generally considered challenging and is not recommended for beginners. It features a significant elevation gain of over 540 meters and includes steep, stony sections that require good physical condition and appropriate footwear. The varied terrain and potential for slippery conditions make it more suitable for experienced joggers or hikers.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on this trail?
faqs.answer: The terrain is quite varied and challenging. You'll encounter wide, well-maintained tracks initially, often following the Río Lago. As you ascend, the path transitions to more mountainous, stony, and steep sections, notably the 'Cuesta Millín'. Expect moraines and loose rocks, typical of its glacial origin. Conditions can be laborious and potentially slippery, especially when wet or icy.

faqs.question: What wildlife or plants can I see along the way?
faqs.answer: The area boasts rich biodiversity. You might spot wildlife such as wolves, foxes, deer, and wild boars, as well as various raptors like black vultures, imperial eagles, and golden eagles. In the waters, alpine newts and Pyrenean desmans can be found. The vegetation ranges from riverside forests of birches, alders, and hazel at lower altitudes to heather, broom, and juniper higher up.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to visit the Lago de Truchillas Trail?
faqs.answer: The best time to visit is typically during late spring, summer, and early autumn. Conditions can vary greatly with the seasons; hot weather in summer can be strenuous, while snow and ice in winter make the trail particularly tough and require specialized equipment. Always check the weather forecast before you go.

faqs.question: What can I see along the route?
faqs.answer: The trail offers breathtaking high-mountain scenery and panoramic views. Key features include the Truchillas River Wooden Bridge and the stunning Lake Truchillas itself, a glacial lake nestled at the foot of Pico Vizcodillo. You'll also pass through glacial valleys, moraines, and diverse vegetation, with expansive views of the surrounding mountains.

faqs.question: Does the trail intersect other well-known paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, while the primary route is the Lago de Truchillas Trail, sections of the path also overlap with parts of the Senda del Lago Truchillas. This allows for a comprehensive experience of the local trail network.

faqs.question: Is the Lago de Truchillas Trail dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: While specific regulations for dogs are not detailed, it's generally advisable to keep dogs on a leash in natural areas, especially given the presence of diverse wildlife, including protected species. Always practice Leave No Trace principles and ensure your dog is capable of handling the challenging terrain and distance.

faqs.question: Do I need a permit or is there an entrance fee for the Sierra de La Cabrera?
faqs.answer: The Lago de Truchillas is designated as a Natural Monument within the Sierra de La Cabrera. Generally, there are no specific permits or entrance fees required to access the trail itself. However, visitors are expected to respect the natural environment and adhere to any local regulations regarding conservation and responsible tourism.

faqs.question: Where does the trail start and is there parking available?
faqs.answer: The trail is a round trip, typically starting and ending near the Río Lago. While specific parking details are not provided, access points are usually found in the vicinity of the Truchillas village. As the area is remote, parking is often informal but generally available near the trailhead. It's recommended to arrive early, especially during peak season, to secure a spot.

faqs.question: What should I pack for jogging this trail?
faqs.answer: Given the trail's difficulty and varied conditions, you should pack appropriate gear. This includes sturdy trail running shoes with good grip, layers of clothing suitable for changing mountain weather, sufficient water and snacks, a map or GPS device (like Komoot's app), sun protection, and a first-aid kit. Trekking poles can also be beneficial for the steeper sections.
